description_long: This filing cabinet is constructed from a series of rectangular, gray, stone-like blocks arranged in a grid pattern, with two vertical columns of drawers. The left column features four smaller square drawers stacked vertically, while the right column contains larger rectangular drawers, some with visible recessed handholds. The surface texture is mottled and matte, resembling concrete or a heavy-duty composite material, and the overall build appears robust and industrial. The drawers are flush with the cabinet face, and there are no visible label holders or external locks. The cabinet's design is utilitarian, likely intended for secure storage in environments where durability is prioritized. Its substantial mass and blocky form suggest it is not easily moved and is meant for stationary use in urban or industrial settings.
description: A robust, gray, stone-textured filing cabinet with two columns of drawers and recessed handholds, designed for heavy-duty storage. Its industrial appearance and solid build make it suitable for secure, stationary placement.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth and stacked arrangement of the drawers, with the left column of four smaller drawers visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying all drawers, including the left column of four small drawers and the right column of larger drawers with recessed handholds.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the depth and the right column of larger drawers, with some handholds visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back panels of the drawers, with no visible handles or openings.
